restated_intent: Create the foundational Verifier primitive that every gate implements: a typed interface with three honest outcomes (PASS, FAIL, CANNOT_DECIDE), two classes (HARD blocks ship, ADVISORY accumulates into a report), and a fail-fast pipeline that short-circuits on the first HARD non-PASS. This makes the #29 bug (model failure laundered as human blame) unrepresentable.

steps.step: Define Outcome enum with PASS, FAIL, CANNOT_DECIDE
covers: every Verdict is JSON-serialisable for the cognition-log

steps.step: Define Klass enum with HARD, ADVISORY
Define frozen Verdict dataclass with gate_id, outcome, klass, score, evidence, diagnosis; enforce ADVISORY invariant in __post_init__
covers: every Verdict is JSON-serialisable for the cognition-log

steps.step: Define Verifier Protocol with gate_id, klass, check(subject, context) -> Verdict
covers: a verifier raising internally is caught and mapped to CANNOT_DECIDE, never PASS

steps.step: Define GateRegistry with hard and advisory lists

Implement run_pipeline(subject, context, reg) -> (bool, list[Verdict]): iterate HARD verifiers, short-circuit on first non-PASS; then iterate ADVISORY verifiers, always appending; wrap each verifier call in try/except mapping to CANNOT_DECIDE
covers: run_pipeline returns (False, verdicts) on the first HARD non-PASS verdict
a verifier raising internally is caught and mapped to CANNOT_DECIDE, never PASS

steps.step: Write tests/test_verify.py covering: (a) HARD short-circuit on FAIL, (b) internal exception → CANNOT_DECIDE, (c) Verdict JSON round-trip
covers: run_pipeline returns (False, verdicts) on the first HARD non-PASS verdict
a verifier raising internally is caught and mapped to CANNOT_DECIDE, never PASS
every Verdict is JSON-serialisable for the cognition-log

steps.step: Run python3 -m unittest discover tests and confirm all green
